close_reason: Correct: async_trait provides dyn dispatch needed for inventory-based backend dispatch
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1229-1238 (SecretStore), 1313-1317 (SigningBackend): both traits use async_trait::async_trait rather than the native async-fn-in-trait stable since Rust 1.75. A future contributor will suggest 'just remove the macro now, AFIT is stable'.

Defense: native AFIT produces unnameable associated future types, which means trait objects (Arc<dyn SecretStore>) can't be constructed without dynosaur, manual DynSecretStore wrappers, or pinning each method to BoxFuture by hand. The whole architecture of this crate is 'callers hold Arc<dyn SecretStore> and pass it around' — the registry pattern (BackendRegistration::factory at lib.rs:1466 returns Arc<dyn SecretStore>) requires it.

The cost of async_trait is one Box per call. For network-bound secret backends (the typical case) this is in the noise.

Defense already in doc comments at lib.rs:1229-1238 — keep them.
title: DEFEND: async_trait over native AFIT for SecretStore/SigningBackend
